findings.where: subgraph NARROW + subgraph FM — cycle 1 draft
findings.detail: Cycle 1 draft included direction TB overrides inside both subgraphs of a flowchart LR root. Project history (topic 1.3) shows this combination produces vertical stacks that collapse the LR side-by-side contrast into a single column on this renderer. Fixed in cycle 2 by removing direction TB from subgraph declarations, allowing LR root to govern layout throughout.

Two-panel LR layout: NARROW subgraph (left, NA→NC chain) contrasts against FM subgraph (right, FB→FA→FO chain). No direction overrides inside subgraphs — Mermaid LR root governs. Node count 5, all within 3-8. All five bold labels are 2-4 words and trace directly to corpus sections 5.1-5.4. RAG appears only as subtitle text (not a bare node label). Governing concept in title: field. No ghost nodes misused.

Many task-specific models → S5.3 narrow AI contrast; Cost paid per task → S5.2/S5.3 training cost contrast; One base model → S5.1/S5.2 definition + trained once; Adaptation layer → S5.4 three methods named without expanding mechanics; Many applications → S5.3 generalisation list. No scope creep into 4.2 (fine-tuning mechanics) or 4.3 (RAG internals).

png_render: skipped — mmdc shell execution not available in this tool context; .mmd written and source-level pre-flight passed

preflight.mermaid_parse: expected pass (syntax matches gold example format exactly; no shell tool available to run mmdc)
preflight.concept_fidelity: pass (all 5 labels trace to corpus S5.1-S5.4; no forward refs to 4.2/4.3 mechanics)
preflight.jargon_label: pass (RAG only in subtitle span, not bare node label)
preflight.title_discipline: pass (governing concept in title: field; no ghost node misuse)
preflight.single_path: pass (each panel is a linear chain; side-by-side panels are sanctioned by intent)
